nodes.context: A powerful 'Ndrangheta clan based in Rosarno, Calabria, involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, extortion, and control of commercial activities.
A criminal and mafia-type organization in Calabria, Italy, of which the Bellocco 'ndrina is a clan.
An 'Ndrangheta clan involved in drug supply through the Port of Gioia Tauro.
An 'Ndrangheta clan allied with the Bellocco, controlling port activities and drug trafficking.
An 'Ndrangheta clan that collaborated with Pesce and Bellocco in controlling port contracts and activities.
A mafia-type organization in Apulia, set up with the involvement of Umberto Bellocco to counter the Camorra.
A mafia-type organization from Campania, Italy, which the SCU was created to counter, but later allied with Bellocco.
A Camorra clan that, with support from Bellocco and Pesce, supplied drugs to Castellammare di Stabia.
An ally of the Bellocco 'ndrina listed in the infobox.
An ally of the Bellocco 'ndrina listed in the infobox.

edges.context: The Bellocco 'ndrina is a clan within the 'Ndrangheta organization.
The Bellocco and Mancuso clans are both involved in drug supply through the Port of Gioia Tauro.
The Bellocco and Pesce clans jointly controlled port activities and drug trafficking, often referred to as the Pesce-Bellocco clan.
The Bellocco and Piromalli-Molè clans collaborated in controlling public contracts for the container terminal at the Port of Gioia Tauro.
Umberto Bellocco was involved in setting up the Sacra Corona Unita, importing 'Ndrangheta codes and structure.
The Sacra Corona Unita was created to counter the growing influence of the Camorra in Apulia.
The Bellocco clan is listed as an ally of the Camorra in the infobox.
The Bellocco and Pesce clans supported the Camorra D'Alessandro clan in a cocaine trafficking scheme.
The Capriati clan is listed as an ally of the Bellocco 'ndrina in the infobox.
The Parisi-Palermiti clan is listed as an ally of the Bellocco 'ndrina in the infobox.
